item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since the establishment of memory isn't integral to the SCP, wouldn't using anti-memetics as the deterrent be simpler/more targeted than amnestics?

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright. Got a few links here. First off, we can go ahead and link the first mention of the UIU to their hub page. Secondly, Agent MacAllister is a reference to Ihp's own UIU Series With No Name Hub, which can also be in-line linked too. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2147 was originally discovered by the Unusual Incidents Unit Cincinnati Division, who were investigating repeated reports of alligators in the sewers of [REDACTED]. It was discovered this was due to an alligator living within SCP-2147 laying eggs in the toilet, with hatchlings occasionally crawling down the drain and living in the sewers. Due to the age of several of the captured alligators, it is estimated that SCP-2147 has existed in some form for at least ten years.

To this:

SCP-2147 was originally discovered by the Unusual Incidents Unit Cincinnati Division, who were investigating repeated reports of alligators in the sewers of [REDACTED]. It was discovered this was due to an alligator living within SCP-2147 laying eggs in the toilet, with hatchlings occasionally crawling down the drain and living in the sewers. Due to the age of several of the captured alligators, it is estimated that SCP-2147 has existed in some form for at least ten years.

Changing this:

Addendum: Interview With UIU Agent MacAllister:

To this:

Addendum: Interview With UIU Agent MacAllister:

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why are there British spellings for words in an article about an American SCP? That…doesn't make any sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I once did a roadtrip to "Live at the Roxy" by Frank Zappa. I understand.

It's spooky, creepy, dangerous and odd. I love it. The spiral expands slowly; first it's an odd house with an everglades problem, by the end it is something truly unique. It's even got a mystery hole!!

+1, Laws yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This skip gives me Jumanji flashbacks, and I have no idea why.

Regardless, loved this in chat, love it here. A+, tenouttaten, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember seeing this one! Overall, it's great; I particularly like how you had a footnote explaining the specific effects of the amnestic class — I'm quite partial to small details of that sort — and that you incorporated the UIU, as it seems that they don't show up in SCPs all that often, though maybe I've overlooked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I totally dig this.

The details about how the living space lines up with the Everglades are great. There's always the risk of swamping the reader in minutiae, but the list of bullet points is the perfect length. It thoroughly develops the object without the reader becoming mired in information.

This article definitely deserves to be preserved as an example of a delightfully weird, spooky SCP object.

Also, I'm inherently partial to pieces with song lyrics in them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, upon parsing that again, I should have been clearer. People who work with that amnestic on a regular basis are inoculated against it, in a way similar to allergy shots: small doses at a time until you build up a resistance against it. I'm going to rewrite the containment procedures within the next day or so, because a lot of complaints seem to come from there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This speaks to me on the level of being an Ohioan with family in Florida. I mean, I read the first line of the description and went "Of course it is!" That said, my one complaint would be the containment procedures; they feel little OTT. I mean, everyone in the Foundation is inoculated against this type of amnestic?

item.title: Re: And Now, The Kingston Trio's Greatest Hits. Also, Author Commentary Pun.
item.content:encoded:
nothing to listen to in the car except Kingston Trio

I once drove across New Mexico with nothing to listen to but an old Donna Summers tape.

It took all the strength I had not to fall apart…er…something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You posted this before I could do my everglades scp D:<

But seriously, I'm heavily biased to like things like this but I think this is very very cool.

item.title: And Now, The Kingston Trio's Greatest Hits. Also, Author Commentary Pun.
item.content:encoded:
This is what happens when you have nothing to listen to in the car except Kingston Trio because your radio is broken. In all honesty, this was me seeing if I could stick a bunch of disparate elements together- a corrupted Kingston Trio song, characters from my UIU series (yes, I will get back to that eventually, for the half of a person who actually read that), spatial fuckery, and the swamp. Plus, don't tell me "Neverglades" isn't a cool word. You are lying.

Thanks to everyone who looked over this in chat. Enjoy!
